sections.heading: What are Android Permissions?
sections.content: Android permissions protect sensitive data and device features. Normal permissions are granted at install, dangerous permissions require runtime approval, and special permissions need system settings access. Since Android 6.0 (API 23), dangerous permissions must be requested at runtime and can be revoked anytime.

sections.heading: Requesting Permissions
items: Show rationale if needed before requesting
Handle result: granted, denied, permanently denied
For special permissions, redirect to system Settings

sections.heading: Best Practices
items: Request permissions when needed, not at launch
Use least privilege and privacy-preserving APIs
Re-check in onResume() in case permissions are revoked

sections.heading: Summary
sections.content: Declare all permissions in the manifest, request dangerous ones at runtime, handle all outcomes, and guide users for special permissions. Follow least privilege, provide rationale, and use privacy-friendly APIs like Photo Picker. Dangerous permissions should be requested contextually and handled gracefully to ensure a good UX.
